notes: Private/JV (SoftBank, OpenAI, Oracle, MGX); NOT a government outlay. 10GW over ~4yr. Aug-2025 SoftBank CFO conceded the venture is taking longer than anticipated.
~EUR150B private (AI Champions Initiative) + ~EUR50B public envelope, of which only ~EUR20B is genuinely new public money for gigafactories (~65-70% private). Public/private NOT summed.
Almost entirely private: Brookfield EUR20B, UAE/MGX up to EUR50B. Announced around the Paris AI Action Summit.

Public Investment Fund (PIF) sovereign-wealth capital deployed as investment, not an appropriated R&D outlay; HUMAIN is the operational arm. Original Bloomberg scoop (2024-11-06, paywalled); corroborated by CIO.com (2024-11-12). No official PIF/HUMAIN release pins the $100B.

$100B is an assets-under-management TARGET for an investment vehicle (Mubadala + G42), not a committed spend. The official Mubadala launch release (2024-03-11) confirms the MGX vehicle and partners but NOT the $100B figure (Bloomberg-sourced).

program: National IC Industry Investment Fund III (Big Fund III)
notes: Semiconductor state fund, AI-adjacent (funds HBM/AI chips). Capital mixes central + local government + SOE banks.

source_name: South China Morning Post / Securities Times
notes: AI-specific early-stage fund; partly drawn from Big Fund III -> OVERLAPS cn-bigfund3-2024, do not sum.
20-year public-private mobilization target spanning AI + quantum + hydrogen; not AI-specific.
Third-party ESTIMATE of cumulative guidance-fund deployment to AI firms 2000-2023 (of ~$912B across all industries). Not an announcement.

program: Sovereign AI Compute Strategy + AI package (Budget 2024)
notes: Appropriated federal outlay over 5yr incl. CAD2B Sovereign Compute. A genuine outlay benchmark.
Public: AIRR/compute (GBP2B+), GBP750M next supercomputer, GBP150-400M chip AMC. Separately GBP28B confirmed private data-centre investment (stored as private_mobilized).
~INR 10,371.92 crore; Cabinet approval 2024-03-07 (per IndiaAI/PIB). Government outlay.
'Up to' EUR500M; government quantum program across 5 firms.
EUR1B over 10 years.
EUR1.8B national quantum plan.
EUR5B public commitment through 2025 (announced 2018, increased from EUR3B to EUR5B in the 2020 update). Confirmed in the official KI-Strategie 2020 update.
Federal quantum-technologies program; official BMFTR release confirms EUR2.18B federal funding.

~KRW9.4tn AI investment by 2027 (a separate ~KRW3.3tn AI fund was also announced). Official joint-ministry AI-Semiconductor Initiative plan (2024-04-25); the KRW9.4tn headline is stated in launch coverage (Korea Herald/korea.net), not the plan PDF text itself.
'More than' SGD1B over 5yr. Official SGPC NAIS 2.0 launch release (2023-12-04); the >SGD1B figure is from launch remarks, not the release text.

source_name: Prime Minister of Australia / Queensland Government
notes: ~AUD940M combined federal + Queensland (~A$470M each, equity + loans) to anchor PsiQuantum's Brisbane facility. Official joint release 2024-04-30.

National AI program. REVISED DOWN: OECD.AI (sourcing the Israel Innovation Authority) puts the budget at ~ILS1B cumulative, not the ~ILS2B previously logged. Plan ~2022; public launch Aug-2023. Outlay figure approximate.
$52.7B appropriated for semiconductor manufacturing/R&D; AI-adjacent (advanced-node fabs produce AI accelerators). A genuine appropriated-outlay benchmark.

program: AI for All — National AI Strategy
notes: Strategy promises over CAD 2.3B for training, adoption, startups, sovereign compute; aims 250,000 jobs by 2031. Multiple re-announcements (CBC, Globe, gov.ca).
£1.1bn plan to back chip firms, boost compute and skills; includes £750M national supercomputer by 2030.
$10B package of AI/data center agreements between Kazakh government, Firebird and NVIDIA.

program: SoftBank French AI data center investment
notes: Up to €75 billion in French AI data centers; also reported by SANA and W.Media.
US government commits $2B to quantum computing; $100M tranches reported to Rigetti, D-Wave.

program: Additional AI investment incl. sovereign chatbot (Lecornu)
notes: €655m additional AI investment announced by PM Lecornu, includes sovereign chatbot for public agents.
China's United Nova $3B plan to build auto wafer foundry.

program: DOE Genesis Mission — AI infrastructure (American Science Cloud, ModCon)
notes: DOE announced ~$320M (Dec 2025) for the Genesis Mission (launched by EO 2025-11-24): American Science Cloud, Transformational AI Models Consortium, foundational AI awards. Federal funds, distinct from Stargate and CHIPS. Exact appropriation source not fully detailed; confidence low; announcement month confirmed, exact day not in source.

program: DOE National Quantum Information Science (QIS) Research Centers — next phase
notes: DOE announced up to $625M over 5 years (2025-11-04) to renew the National QIS Research Centers. Only $125M (FY2025) currently committed; outyears contingent on appropriations — public_outlay_usd reflects the committed $125M, headline the up-to-$625M ceiling. Distinct from NQI reauthorization.

program: NSF National AI Research Resource (NAIRR) Operations Center
notes: NSF announced up to $35M (2025-09-03) for one org to operate the NAIRR Operations Center for up to 5 years, transitioning NAIRR from pilot to permanent. Modest federal AI infrastructure award.

program: AI Continent Action Plan / AI Factories (EuroHPC)
notes: AI Continent Action Plan (Apr 2025) cites ~€10B for the AI Factories network 2021-2027 via EuroHPC JU. Distinct from InvestAI €200B/€20B gigafactories (already ledgered as eu-investai). EU + member-state co-funded HPC.

Commission to mobilise ~€1B from existing EU programmes (Horizon Europe, Digital Europe) to accelerate AI adoption across strategic sectors (announced 2025-10-08). Funds drawn from existing envelopes — possible partial overlap; medium confidence.

£2.5B government R&D over 10 years from 2024 (published 2023-03-20 with Budget 2023). Distinct from UK AI Opportunities Action Plan (already ledgered). Slightly pre-dates the 3-year window but fills a UK-quantum gap. Some sources cite ~£1B private co-investment ambition — only government portion in public_outlay_usd.

£1.1B AI Hardware Plan unveiled 2026-06-08 at London Tech Week: £750M national supercomputer by 2030, £400M next-gen chips, £120M chip design, £45M skills. Recency item. Possible partial overlap with uk-compute-roadmap (£750M supercomputer) — flagged.

~€18B (floor) innovation initiative adopted Jul 2025 by the Federal Ministry of Research, Technology & Space; six key techs incl. AI, quantum, microelectronics, fusion, biotech. Distinct from Germany 2018 AI Strategy (de-ai-strategy). Headline approximate; low confidence on exact envelope/horizon.

EU-approved €5B German State aid for the ESMC Dresden fab (total project ~€10B) under the EU Chips Act; ground-broken 2024-08-20. public_outlay_usd is the €5B subsidy only; ~€10B total includes private JV capex (NOT summed).

program: STMicroelectronics-GlobalFoundries Crolles fab — France 2030 state aid
notes: ~€2.9B French State aid (France 2030) toward the ~€7.5B Crolles 300mm FD-SOI fab; finalized 2023-06-05 under the EU Chips Act. public_outlay_usd is the French subsidy only. CAVEAT: GlobalFoundries later paused its portion (2025); subsidy partially on hold. Secondary source — low confidence.

Spain's 2024 AI Strategy (approved 2024-05-14); €1.5B from the Recovery, Transformation & Resilience Plan for 2024-2025 (additional to ~€600M under the 2020 ENIA — not summed). Includes a MareNostrum upgrade.

program: CDP Venture Capital national AI fund
notes: €1B state fund via CDP Venture Capital (state lender CDP), announced 2024-03-12 by PM Meloni; up to €2B additional private capital stored separately in private_mobilized_usd (NOT summed). Targets startups, scale-ups, a national Italian LLM.

program: AI and Semiconductor Industry Strengthening Framework (public support)
notes: METI 'AI & Semiconductor Industry Strengthening Framework' in the Nov 2024 economic package: >¥10T public support by FY2030 (Rapidus, TSMC, Micron). The SEPARATE ¥50T public-private mobilization target is a companion row (jp-ai-semi-50T-mobilize) — do NOT sum. usd_approx at ~150 JPY/USD.

program: AI and Semiconductor Industry Strengthening Framework (public-private mobilization)
notes: Headline >¥50T public-private investment over 10 years (~¥160T targeted economic impact). MOBILIZATION TARGET, not appropriated spend. The genuine public portion is the ¥10T in companion record jp-ai-semi-framework-2024 — public_outlay_usd left null HERE to avoid double-counting. usd_approx at ~150 JPY/USD.

Up to ¥590B (~$3.89B) additional subsidy approved Apr 2024 for Rapidus 2nm mass production by 2027. Distinct tranche; later rounds (Mar 2025, Feb 2026) are additional and tracked separately to avoid double-count.

W150tr (~$109B) public-private National Growth Fund over 5 yrs (President Lee Jae-myung). Structured as W75tr govt-guaranteed + W75tr private — shown separately, NOT summed. Priority incl. AI semiconductor foundry, AI chips. usd_approx at ~1380 KRW/USD.

W26tr (~$19B) record chip support package (May 2024, President Yoon), incl. W8.8tr low-interest financing + Yongin cluster infrastructure. Separate from private fab capex at Yongin (not counted). usd_approx at ~1380 KRW/USD.

program: Ten Major AI Infrastructure Projects ('AI island')
notes: NT$100B (~$3.1B) for 'Ten Major AI Infrastructure Projects' (data centers, quantum hubs, AI robotics labs). The cited NT$15T by 2040 is an economic-impact projection, NOT investment (excluded). Govt-vs-VC split unclear; low confidence. usd_approx at ~32 TWD/USD.

Union Cabinet approved the National Quantum Mission 2023-04-19, ₹6,003.65 crore (~$730M) for 2023-24 to 2030-31 (50-1000 qubit machines, 4 thematic hubs). Distinct from IndiaAI Mission (already ledgered). Slightly pre-dates the 3-year window but fills an India-quantum gap.

$10B AI-focused VC fund by PIF-backed HUMAIN (the PIF AI national champion launched 2025-05-12). Sovereign-wealth-backed venture capital, not public budget outlay — in private_mobilized_usd. Distinct from Project Transcendence (sa-transcendence). Low confidence pending formal close.

$20B JV between Qai (QIA subsidiary, Qatar's national AI company) and Brookfield for AI infrastructure in Qatar + select intl markets (2025-12-09, verified via QIA). Sovereign-wealth + private asset-manager mobilization, not appropriated budget — in private_mobilized_usd.

R$23B national AI plan presented to President Lula 2024-07-30 (sovereign supercomputer, workforce, public-sector AI, research). Multi-year 2024-2028; mix of government + state-bank financing. usd_approx at ~0.182 BRL/USD.

program: National AI Action Plan 2026-2030 / data-centre roadmap
notes: Recency: ~$3B public funds for data-centre + AI under the 2026-2030 roadmap; govt to direct >=2% of public investment to AI. Separately seeking up to $10B private (NOT summed). Press-reported, no primary gov URL yet — low confidence.

program: Intel federal equity stake (CHIPS grant conversion + Secure Enclave)
notes: US govt invested $8.9B in Intel common stock — 433.3M shares (9.9%) at $20.47 — funded by $5.7B remaining CHIPS grants (awarded-but-not-paid) + $3.2B DoD Secure Enclave. Total federal support ~$11.1B incl. prior $2.2B CHIPS disbursed. REPURPOSED CHIPS money converted to sovereign equity rather than fresh appropriation. Landmark instrument shift: grants -> sovereign equity.

program: CHIPS and Science Act — Division B science authorizations (NSF/NIST/DOE/EDA)
notes: AUTHORIZED only (~$174B per CRS framing; CSIS cites ~$200B incl. NSF $81B, DOE $67.5B, NIST ~$10B, EDA $10B) over FY2023-2027 — largely NEVER appropriated. Authorization differs from appropriation. status=authorized, public_outlay_usd=null. Major double-count source for the '$280B CHIPS' headline (the ~$52B incentives are the appropriated portion; this is the unappropriated remainder).

source_name: BetaKit — What's in Budget 2025 for Canadian tech
notes: CAD $925.6M over 5 yrs (from 2025-26) for sovereign public AI compute. LARGELY REANNOUNCED: $800M of the $925.6M is drawn from Budget 2024's $2B Sovereign AI Compute Strategy — only ~$125.6M is genuinely new (amount_low). event_key REUSES ca-budget-2024-ai to flag the double-count (will NOT be double-summed). Tabled Nov 4 2025 under Carney govt.

source_name: BetaKit — What's in Budget 2025 for Canadian tech
notes: CAD $334.3M over 5 yrs for quantum, split ~$223.1M research + ~$111.2M industry/defence adoption. Tabled Nov 4 2025. Appears to be new money (distinct from 2021 National Quantum Strategy).

source_name: ISED — Second phase of the Pan-Canadian AI Strategy
notes: CAD >$443M committed in Budget 2021, Phase 2 launched 22 Jun 2022, led by CIFAR. Allocations: CIFAR $160M talent + $48M renewal, $40M compute, $60M to AI institutes (Amii/Mila/Vector), $125M Global Innovation Clusters. Multi-year, partially disbursed. Verified via ISED primary release.

Incremental CAD $360M over 7 yrs starting 2021-22 (committed Budget 2021), strategy launched 13 Jan 2023, coordinated by ISED. Allocations: ~$141M research, ~$45M talent, ~$169M commercialization. Multi-year, partially disbursed. Verified via ISED primary page.

€43B 'mobilized' public+private target to 2030; EU-budget-only share ~€3.3B (Horizon Europe + Digital Europe) in public_outlay_usd. Bruegel (May 2026): only ~€13.75B state aid approved by Dec 2025. Public and private NEVER summed; private_mobilized = remaining ~€39.7B. Underdelivered vs target.

~€7B total (2021-2027), ~€3B from EU budget (Horizon Europe, Digital Europe, CEF) plus member-state co-funding — all public. Actively deploying supercomputers, quantum computers, and AI factories. event_key SHARED with the AI Factories/AI Continent row (eu-ai-factories-2025) since the AI Factories money runs through EuroHPC — deduplicated, never double-summed. Strong disbursement transparency.

€7.6B headline (2021-2027) across supercomputing, AI, cybersecurity, digital skills, deployment. AI subset ~€2.1B. NOTE: partial conceptual overlap with EU Chips Act / EuroHPC funding sources (both draw on Digital Europe) — kept distinct as a named programme; do not sum naively.

source_name: Brussels Signal — Intel cancels German chip factory (Jul 2025)
notes: €9.9B German state aid pledged (2022) for a ~€30B Intel megafab; CANCELLED 24 Jul 2025 under CEO Lip-Bu Tan. Subsidy NEVER drawn down — public_outlay_usd = 0. Canonical example of a cancelled commitment (announced != delivered).

program: Infineon Dresden Smart Power Fab (IPCEI ME/CT state aid)
notes: €920M German federal aid under IPCEI ME/CT / EU Chips Act for the Dresden Smart Power Fab; construction began Mar 2023, fab opening planned 2026. Infineon itself investing ~€5B (private, not summed). EC approval 2025.
€615M total from the Dutch National Growth Fund (PPP grant), phased: initial commitment Apr 2021, Phase 2 €228M (Apr 2022), Phase 3 €273M (Jul 2024). Disbursing.

program: AiNed national AI programme (National Growth Fund)
source_name: National Growth Fund / Dutch AI Coalition — AiNed
notes: Up to €276M from the National Growth Fund for the national AI programme (AIC4NL); first round Apr 2021. ~€189M implemented by Sept 2025 (public_outlay reflects the implemented ~€189M). Headline €276M is the conditional ceiling.

source_name: EE Times — Broadcom Cancels Spain Fab
notes: €12.25B public investment (revised up from €11B), approved 24 May 2022, to 2027, EU-recovery-funded. SETT approved ~€2B co-investments by 2025 but a leading-edge fab was NOT secured — Broadcom cancelled its ~$1B back-end plant (mid-2025). Weak outcomes -> stalled.

PIF-owned full-stack AI company; announced Feb 2025, launched 12 May 2025. ~$100B programmatic AI commitment; plans ~6.6GW data centers, deploys Nvidia GPUs, co-invests globally. SWF mandate = target, not budget; public_outlay null. DISTINCT from sa-humain-vcfund ($10B VC sub-fund).

PIF-owned vehicle launched Feb 2024 with a $100B-by-2030 mandate across electronics/advanced industrials incl. semiconductors. SWF target, not budget; public_outlay null. By Apr 2026 Alat reportedly DROPPED its chip-manufacturing ambition and removed its CEO (Semafor) — status flagged 'stalled' for the semiconductor angle; mandate figure retained as originally announced.

$1.5B corporate equity investment by Microsoft into the Abu Dhabi state-linked AI firm G42; Brad Smith joins the G42 board. Announced/closed Apr 2024. Private capital into a state-linked recipient — public_outlay null.

QR 9B (~$2.5B; trade.gov cites $2.4B) incentive package for AI/technology/innovation & digital transformation, announced May 2024 by the PM. DISTINCT from qa-qai-brookfield. Headline in QAR; usd_approx uses $2.5B.

METI approved an additional ¥631.5B for Rapidus on 11 Apr 2026 for 2nm R&D / IIM-1. Converted at 150 JPY/USD = ~$4.21B (Reuters cite ~$3.96B at spot ~159). Cumulative gov R&D support ~¥2.35T after this tranche. DISTINCT from jp-rapidus-subsidy-apr2024.

₹76,000 crore outlay (2021); fiscal support up to 50% of project cost for fabs + DLI. 10 projects approved (incl. Tata-PSMC Gujarat fab ₹91,526cr, Tata Assam, Micron, CG Power). usd_approx ~$9.1B (range $9-10B). DISTINCT from in-indiaai and in-nqm.

program: National Quantum Strategy / National Reconstruction Fund (critical tech)
source_name: Dept. of Industry, Science & Resources — National Quantum Strategy
notes: National Quantum Strategy released May 2023; min. A$1B of the A$15B National Reconstruction Fund earmarked for critical technologies (incl. quantum). A$1B x 0.66 = ~$660M. DISTINCT from au-psiquantum. Confidence low: A$1B is a critical-tech earmark, not quantum-exclusive.

program: Safe & Responsible AI / National AI Centre (2024-25 Budget)
notes: 2024-25 Budget: A$39.9M over 5 yrs for safe & responsible AI adoption, incl. A$21.6M to establish/reshape the National AI Centre + AI advisory body, A$15.7M policy/regulation, A$2.6M national security. A$39.9M x 0.66 = ~$26.3M.
